name: Date-Nut Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
COOKIE DOUGH:
1 cup butter -- room temperature
2 cups packed brown sugar
2 eggs
1 tablespoon lemon juice
1 teaspoon vanilla
4 cups all purpose flour -- sifted
1 teaspoon baking soda
1 teaspoon salt
Mix together all filling ingredients in a small saucepan; cook over medium/low heat until the dates are soft. Allow to cool, then set aside. Make the dough by creaming together the butter and sugar in a large bowl until light and fluffy. Add the eggs; beat well. Incorporate the lemon juice and vanilla; stir. Sift the flour, baking soda, and salt into the mixture. Sift again. Combine with the creamed mixture; mix well. The mixture will be somewhat soft. Divide the dough into two portions; place each on a well-floured surface. Roll each out into rectangles about 1/2 inch thick. Spread evenly with the filling. Roll up as for a jelly roll. Cover with plastic wrap. Slice into 1/8 to 1/4 thick pieces with a sharp knife. Place on greased cookie sheets. Bake at 400°F for about 12 minutes, or until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack. (Cookies freeze well.) Yield: 4-5 dozen.
